headline: Grid Costs: No Shifting
full_summary: This bill likely aims to prevent large energy consumers, such as data centers, from disproportionately shifting their electrical grid usage costs onto residential and smaller commercial customers. It seeks to ensure that entities with high energy demands contribute more directly to the infrastructure and maintenance costs of the power grid, thereby promoting a fairer distribution of utility expenses among all rate payers.
supporters: Consumer advocates, residential customers, small businesses, environmental groups (seeking efficiency incentives).
opponents: Data center industry, large industrial energy users, economic development organizations.

headline: Track Data Center Water Use
full_summary: This legislation proposes to mandate reporting on water consumption by data centers. Recognizing the significant water usage by these facilities for cooling, the bill aims to increase transparency and provide data for better resource management, environmental monitoring, and sustainability planning. It would require data center operators to disclose their water usage metrics to a designated state authority.
supporters: Environmental organizations, water conservation groups, local communities, public health advocates.
opponents: Data center industry (due to administrative burden or proprietary concerns), certain business advocacy groups.

headline: Limit Data Center Noise, Siting
full_summary: This bill addresses community concerns regarding the physical placement (siting) and noise pollution generated by data centers. It likely proposes new regulations or strengthens existing ones to control where data centers can be developed, particularly in relation to residential areas, and establishes specific limits on operational noise levels. The intent is to mitigate adverse impacts on local quality of life and ensure more harmonious community development.
supporters: Local residents, community associations, environmental justice groups, local government planning departments.
opponents: Data center industry, real estate developers, economic development agencies (concerned about restrictions).
